item.text:
In the leaking version, `self.calls` keeps every allowed request timestamp forever. `allow()` only needs timestamps inside the current sliding window, so expired entries should be removed before checking `len(self.calls)`. Without that pruning, memory grows with total historical traffic instead of bounded recent traffic.

The minimal fix is to assign the filtered list back before enforcing the limit:

```python
def allow(self) -> bool:
    now = time.time()
    self.calls = [t for t in self.calls if now - t < self.window]
    if len(self.calls) < self.limit:
        self.calls.append(now)
        return True
    return False
```

Patch form:

```diff
 def allow(self) -> bool:
     now = time.time()
+    self.calls = [t for t in self.calls if now - t < self.window]
     if len(self.calls) < self.limit:
         self.calls.append(now)
         return True
     return False
```

In this workspace, [demo/rate_limiter.py](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-uglee-Coding-ralphton-research/16c236c5-e508-4436-9d28-e8588d7300ac/scratchpad/omb-grid-hook/demo/rate_limiter.py:15) already has that exact pruning line, so the file appears to be fixed already.
